title: AreaOfInterest
description:
Definitions for the three Area of Interest variants used by SEPAL recipes. This schema does NOT define a top-level type — recipes compose their own `oneOf` from the variants they support (e.g. radarMosaic uses eeTable + polygon; the asset recipe also allows assetBounds).

AOI selection has direct cost implications: smaller AOIs are faster and cheaper in Earth Engine; very large AOIs (continental scale) may hit memory or compute limits depending on what processing the recipe applies. When suggesting AOIs to a user, prefer the smallest area that captures their region of interest.

countryTableId.description: Earth Engine asset id of SEPAL's built-in country/area table — a derivative of the FAO GAUL (Global Administrative Unit Layers) dataset. Always use this exact id when selecting countries or sub-national administrative areas.

eeTable.title: AoiEeTable
eeTable.description:
AOI defined by selecting one or more rows from an Earth Engine FeatureCollection table. The geometry is the union of the selected features. There are two distinct cases, discriminated by `id`:

1. **Country / administrative-area selection** (`id` = `users/wiell/SepalResources/gaul`). Use the `aoi_list_countries` and `aoi_list_country_areas` tools to obtain the integer ADM_CODE that goes into `key`. The country branch additionally requires `level` (`COUNTRY` or `AREA`) and forces `keyColumn = 'id'`.

2. **User-supplied table** (`id` is any other FeatureCollection asset). Filter by a column value (`keyColumn` + `key`, both strings) or include every row (`keyColumn: null`, `key: null`). The `level` field is not allowed in this case.

The optional `buffer` enlarges the geometry by a number of kilometers — useful for some surrounding context (coastline overflow, transboundary features), but increases the AOI area roughly in proportion.

type.description: Discriminator. Must be the literal string 'EE_TABLE'.

id.description: Earth Engine asset id of the FeatureCollection. For country/area selection, this MUST be `users/wiell/SepalResources/gaul`. For a user-supplied table, any FeatureCollection asset id the user has read access to (typically `projects/<project-id>/assets/<table-name>` or `users/<user>/<asset-path>`).

keyColumn.description:
Name of the column to filter by. When set, only rows where `keyColumn = key` are included. When `null`, every row in the table is included.

For the SEPAL country table, this MUST be `'id'`. For user tables, any string column name in the table's schema.

key.description:
Value to match in `keyColumn`. The accepted type depends on which case applies:

- **Country/area selection** (`id` is the country table): an **integer** ADM_CODE from FAO GAUL — e.g. `73` for Ecuador, `21` for Brazil. Resolve via `aoi_list_countries` / `aoi_list_country_areas`. Never an ISO alpha-3 string.
- **User table with `keyColumn` set**: a non-empty string matching that column's values.
- **User table with `keyColumn = null`**: must also be `null`.

level.description:
Distinguishes country-level from sub-national area-level selection in the SEPAL country table. **Required** when `id` is the country table; **forbidden** in all other cases.

- `'COUNTRY'` — `key` is a country ADM_CODE, geometry is the entire country.
- `'AREA'` — `key` is an area (province/state/district) ADM_CODE, geometry is that single area.

buffer.description:
Buffer distance in **kilometers** applied around the selected geometry. Default 0 (no buffer). Useful when the AOI boundary is precise (e.g. an administrative boundary) but the user wants to capture features just outside (coastlines, transboundary forest patches, etc.).

Larger buffers proportionally enlarge the area Earth Engine has to process. Any integer in [0, 100] is valid; the GUI presents typical values 0, 1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100.

if.description: Country/area branch when id is the SEPAL country table.

then.description: Country/area selection: keyColumn must be 'id', key is the integer GAUL ADM_CODE, level is required.

else.description: User-supplied table: level is not allowed; keyColumn and key must both be set (strings) or both be null.

allOf.description: keyColumn and key must both be set or both be null.

polygon.title: AoiPolygon
polygon.description:
AOI defined by an explicit polygon. Use this when the user wants to draw a custom region that doesn't correspond to a country, administrative area, or asset.

Coordinate order is **[longitude, latitude]** (GeoJSON convention), not [lat, lng]. Coordinates are in WGS84 decimal degrees. The polygon does not need to be explicitly closed — the first and last points need not match. Polygons should not self-intersect.

type.description: Discriminator. Must be the literal string 'POLYGON'.

path.description: Array of `[longitude, latitude]` points forming a closed polygon (the polygon is implicitly closed — do not duplicate the first point as the last). Minimum three points. Coordinates are in WGS84 decimal degrees: longitude in [-180, 180], latitude in [-90, 90].

assetBounds.title: AoiAssetBounds
assetBounds.description: AOI defined by the bounding geometry of the recipe's input asset. This variant is only valid for recipe types that wrap a single bounding asset (currently only the `asset` recipe type) — the geometry is implicit in the asset itself, so no further fields are needed.

type.description: Discriminator. Must be the literal string 'ASSET_BOUNDS'.
